title: OGP Digest | June 2026
content:
Towards a Collective Ownership of AI | By Henri Verdier, Director INRIA Foundation

AI is no longer just a disruptive technology: it is reshaping work, education, and democratic life itself. Yet this revolution remains driven by a handful of companies, with no structured international governance to ensure it serves human dignity or democratic values. Henri Verdier argues that the trajectory of AI must be shaped by all of us, through open, inclusive, and participatory governance, before the systems deployed today become tomorrow's infrastructure. More here.

Martin Tisné Awarded Chevalier de l'Ordre National du Mérite

OGP founder and friend Martin Tisné has been awarded the Chevalier de l'Ordre National du Mérite, presented personally by French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace. President Macron referenced OGP several times in crediting Tisné's many achievements, a testament to the lasting impact of his work on open government. A well-deserved honour, and a proud moment for everyone across the OGP community. More here.

Launch of Democracy27
snippet: Towards a Collective Ownership of AI | By Henri Verdier, Director INRIA Foundation

title: OGP Digest | May 2026
content:
Open Gov Week 2026 marks the commitment that reformers around the world are making to transparency, accountability, and participation. From 18–22 May 2026, More than 2,000 events took place across 40+ countries. More here.

15 Years of OGP

Fifteen years ago, OGP started with eight founding countries. Now, it has grown into a global movement of 73 countries, more than 135 local governments, and thousands of civil society organizations. This milestone is both a recognition of what has been built and a call to go further by scaling ambitious reforms, strengthening democratic institutions, and advancing solutions on issues such as digital governance, civic space, economic transparency, and public service delivery. More here.

Towards Open, Resilient and Prosperous Futures: Open Letter from Aidan Eyakuze, CEO of OGP

Hear from our CEO, Aidan Eyakuze, on how a simple idea 15 years ago has turned into over 5,600 reforms across a global network dedicated to making open, transparent, and participatory governance possible. More here.

Global Partnerships Summit
snippet: Open Gov Week 2026 marks the commitment that reformers around the world are making to transparency, accountability, and participation. From 18–22 May 2026, More than 2,000 events took place across 40+

title: OGP Digest | April 2026
content:
15 years of Open Government

As OGP marks its 15th anniversary during Open Gov Week (18–22 May), we're putting the spotlight on the community behind the progress, share a one-minute video of the reform you're most proud of, or your vision for the future of open government. On social media, post a video using #OpenGovWeek and tag OGP to help showcase what this community has achieved and where it's going next.  Download social media toolkit.

OGP at the OECD-OCDE Global Anti-Corruption & Integrity Forum

At the OECD Global Anti-Corruption & Integrity Forum, OGP CEO Aidan Eyakuze and OGP’s Director of Partnerships Alessandro Bellantoni contributed to roundtable discussions ahead of the UK's Illicit Finance Summit and to ongoing exchanges with The World Bank Group's Beneficial Ownership Transparency Hub, while engaging with the President of the Dominican Republic, Luis Abinader, and Thailand's Deputy Prime Minister, Borwornsak Uwanno, in support of both countries' reform agendas. More here.

Guatemala Action Plan Launch

title: OGP Digest | March 2026
content:
Save the Date!

Open Gov week will be held from May 18-22.

Every year, Open Gov Week brings the global open government community together from governments and civil society to parliaments, justice, academia, and the private sector to share ideas, spark action, and celebrate progress. This year is special: 2026 marks 15 years of the Open Government Partnership. 15 years of collaboration between governments and civil society to build more open, resilient, and prosperous societies. Be part of the movement and stay tuned for how you can join in!

Democracy Narratives: What Works and What Doesn’t

March 24, 9:00 AM 10:30 AM EST

Register here

Join the Democracy Narratives Alliance for the release of new global research on how narratives can strengthen democracy—and why this matters now more than ever. The new set of research materials synthesizes nearly 400 academic and practitioner publications, evidence from over 150 studies, and insights from more than 30 international organizations and funders. More here.

Towards an Open Africa: African Open Government Forum
snippet:
Save the Date!

Open Gov week will be held from May 18-22.

title: OGP Digest | February 2026
content:
Launch of New OGP Action Plans

Spain, Montenegro, Armenia, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, New Zealand, Peru, Ecuador, Uruguay, and Argentina have all launched their new OGP action plans, advancing transparency, accountability, and citizen participation in their countries.

Simplification and Open Government Agenda: Friends or Foes?

Governments across Europe are advancing simplification reforms to reduce red tape and improve public services, but these efforts can sometimes risk transparency, accountability, and public participation. Experts from OGP member countries will share practical examples and insights on how openness, digitalization, and citizen engagement can make simplification more effective and democratic. Join the OGP Europe Dialogue webinar on March 12, 3–4:30 pm CET. Register here.

OGP Transparency Fellowship
snippet: Launch of New OGP Action Plans

title: OGP Digest | January 2026
content:
This year marks OGP’s 15th anniversary. While we celebrate this milestone, we also recognize that the world has changed in some difficult ways since our founding in 2011. Now more than ever, we turn to our members and the power of co-creation to meet this critical moment.

Looking ahead, we will draw on lessons learned from across our global community, where reformers continue to inspire change despite shrinking budgets and increasingly complex environments. In 2026, we will continue to support the work of open government champions on the ground and to share examples of success that show how governments and citizens are working together to advance open government and deliver meaningful, lasting impact.

Aidan Eyakuze

Thailand Joins the Open Government Partnership
snippet: This year marks OGP’s 15th anniversary. While we celebrate this milestone, we also recognize that the world has changed in some difficult ways since our founding in 2011. Now more than ever, we turn

title: OGP Digest | December 2025
content:
The Government of the United Kingdom and Laura Neuman to become 2026-27 OGP Co-Chairs

As the Government of Brazil and Steph Muchai begin their tenure as Co-Chairs of the OGP Steering Committee, OGP is equally thrilled to welcome the Government of the United Kingdom and Laura Neuman as incoming Co-Chairs for 2026–2027. More here.

Strengthening Openness across Francophone Africa

The Support Program for Francophone Open Governments and France’s Institut national du service public (ISP) officially handed over four new digital training modules to the École nationale d'administration (ENA) of Côte d’Ivoire, Senegal, and Tunisia. More here.

Apolitical Course: How to be Transparent, Participatory and Accountable

In partnership with Apolitical, OGP has launched an interactive course on open government to give civil servants the tools and knowledge to make government more transparent, participatory, and accountable. In under 4 hours, you’ll learn to:
🔷 Understand the benefits of open government
🔷 Apply its principles in your work
🔷 Connect open government to the policy areas you care about
🔷 Take practical steps to make a real difference

Interested in taking part? More here.
snippet: The Government of the United Kingdom and Laura Neuman to become 2026-27 OGP Co-Chairs

title: OGP Digest | November 2025
content:
Launch of 2025-2026 Co-Chair Agenda

At COP30, the Government of Brazil and Steph Muchai officially launched their 2025–2026 Co-Chair Agenda for OGP, a bold vision for how open government can respond to today’s global challenges. This agenda is a call to action to rebuild trust, protect civic space, and deliver more just, resilient, and democratic societies. More here.

Emily O’Reilly Appointed as Open Government Partnership Ambassador

The Open Government Partnership is thrilled to announce that Emily O’Reilly, former European Ombudsman, has been confirmed to serve as an OGP Ambassador. “The work of OGP has never been more vital,” notes O’Reilly. “My ambition is to replace narratives of hate and polarization with a hope that is built on action, and visible in people’s everyday lives.” More here.

Open Government at COP30: Integrity, Transparency and Social Participation to Strengthen Climate Policies

title: OGP Global Summit Recap | October 2025
content:
Thank you to everyone who attended the 2025 OGP Summit! Between Oct 6-10, 2000+ participants from over 70 countries gathered in Vitoria-Gasteiz, Spain to share ideas, collaborate, and explore how open government can rebuild trust and strengthen democracy. Over three days, delegates engaged with 384 speakers across four plenaries, eight high-level panels, five global dialogues, 40 breakout sessions, and 50+ side events covering topics like public integrity, ethics, digital governance, open justice, and civic participation.

Browse the full photo gallery. More here.

Relive the plenary sessions on our Youtube channel.

Take five minutes to fill out our latest OGP Stakeholder Survey. Let us know how the OGP platform and services are working for you. Your feedback helps us improve our support and better serve our community. More here.
snippet: Thank you to everyone who attended the 2025 OGP Summit! Between Oct 6-10, 2000+ participants from over 70 countries gathered in Vitoria-Gasteiz, Spain to share ideas, collaborate, and explore how open

title: Local Roundup | October 2025
content:
"Local governments must move from deciding alone to being part of a State that shares its decisions, is guided by citizens, and embraces shared responsibility. An open government in action means a living democracy." Watch the editorial.

Want more news? Read the full Roundup on our website! More here.

Learn how Spain’s OGP Local members are embracing reforms to make public information easier to understand. More here.

Madrid (Spain) celebrated the implementation of a public investment policy that targets vulnerable districts, which led to the adoption of over 300 projects co-designed with civil society. The city also held a second co-creation workshop on “Youth Collectives in Disadvantaged Neighbourhoods” with THIVIC Social Innovation Lab to map challenges and opportunities for young people and co-create inclusive solutions. More here and here.

Bogotá (Colombia) is advancing public participation by establishing a new channel for citizens' engagement on garbage management, developing a deliberative participation assembly, and holding the Local Communal Congress to foster dialogue and participation. More here, here, and here.
